## Summary

**Root cause:** Pronoun inconsistency detector flagged group/collective "they/them" references as pronoun shifts. When a scene says "they all went to the beach" meaning a group, the LLM recorded they/them for individual characters, triggering false `pronoun_inconsistency` warnings.

**Fix:**
- **Prompt:** Explicit pronoun guidance — distinguish individual they/them from collective group-referent they. Record individual pronouns only, not group reference pronouns.
- **Reducer:** Smarter inconsistency detection. If they/them coexists with a single consistent gendered pronoun across chunks, treat it as collective reference noise and don't flag it. Only flags genuine multi-pronoun variations.
- **Renderer:** Shows explanatory note about collective reference filtering when no shifts are detected.

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/prompts/pass1a-character-sweep.ts` — pronoun guidance section
- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/characterReducer.ts` — smarter pronoun inconsistency logic
- `components/ledger/StoryLedgerLayers.tsx` — collective reference note in Identity layer

**Does NOT touch:**
- No role taxonomy changes (A1)
- No POV changes (A2)
- No relationship changes (A3)
- No symbol changes (A4)
- No scoring, report renderer, or Revise workbench changes

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- The collective reference filter only applies when they/them appears alongside a single consistent gendered pronoun. Genuine non-binary characters who use they/them exclusively are unaffected.
- Characters with true multi-pronoun usage (e.g. he/they) will still be flagged correctly.
- Backward compatible: old data triggers same warnings as before unless they/them + gendered pronoun pattern is present.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: N/A — changes pronoun detection logic within existing Phase 1A reducer. No new pipeline stages.
